entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A critical input for crop production.

entities.name: NITROGEN USE INEFFICIENCIES
entities.description: Conditions resulting in substantial economic losses and environmental externalities.

entities.name: NITRATE LEACHING
entities.description: An environmental externality resulting from nitrogen use inefficiencies.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITRIFICATION INHIBITION
entities.description: A plant-mediated mechanism that suppresses soil nitrification via root-secreted compounds to improve nitrogen retention.

entities.name: SOIL NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The process of converting ammonia to nitrate in soil, which is suppressed by BNI.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The cereal crop being evaluated for the potential of biological nitrification inhibition.

entities.name: EX-ANTE CROP SIMULATION
entities.description: An approach used to evaluate the potential agronomic and environmental benefits of BNI.

entities.name: DSSAT
entities.description: A crop system model applied to simulate sorghum systems.

entities.name: AHMEDNAGAR DISTRICT
entities.description: The specific district in Maharashtra, India, used as a representative location for the sorghum system.

entities.name: MAHARASHTRA
entities.description: The state in India where the study's representative system is located.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study's representative system is located.

entities.name: CSH-5
entities.description: A widely cultivated sorghum hybrid used in the simulation.

entities.name: BNI EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The varying level of effectiveness of biological nitrification inhibition in virtual cultivars.

entities.name: SORGHUM YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the sorghum crop, which can be increased by high-efficiency BNI.

entities.name: NITROGEN LOSSES
entities.description: The loss of nitrogen from the agroecosystem, which can be reduced by BNI.
